metadata.note: One ticket (#64990) has been reopened after appearing in the closed-query snapshot. Four tickets remain blocked from full review.

tickets.title: Add filters for input and output validation
tickets.summary: Adds `wp_ability_validate_input` and `wp_ability_validate_output`, allowing extenders to augment or replace the default JSON Schema validation result for ability inputs and outputs. Follow-up commits preserve custom REST error statuses and stop HTML-escaping validated ability names in JSON-bound errors.
tickets.documentation_rationale: New public validation filters and REST error-handling behavior need developer documentation, but fit best in a combined Abilities API Dev Note.

tickets.title: Improve JSON Schema compatibility when handling abilities
tickets.summary: Introduces AI-compatible compilation of ability JSON Schemas, normalizing structural keywords and provider constraints while preserving canonical WordPress validation. It also adds schema extensibility and REST schema keyword support needed by AI clients and adapters.
tickets.documentation_rationale: This is a substantial new developer-facing schema compilation API with interoperability and compatibility implications. It requires a dedicated Dev Note with examples and provider-target guidance.

tickets.title: The `core/get-site-info` ability returns wrong locale code in the admin
tickets.summary: Corrects the locale returned by `core/get-site-info` in administrative contexts.
tickets.documentation_rationale: Narrow bug fix restoring expected locale data; no new API or migration guidance.

tickets.title: Add execution lifecycle filters to WP_Ability methods
tickets.summary: Adds execution-lifecycle extensibility to `WP_Ability`, including input normalization, permission-result filtering, pre-execution short-circuiting, and execution-result filtering.
tickets.documentation_rationale: Multiple new public hooks alter how plugins can participate in ability execution and require ordering, error-handling, and usage examples.

tickets.title: Add filtering support to `wp_get_abilities()`
tickets.summary: A first implementation added filtering by category, namespace, and meta, caller callbacks, ecosystem filters, and REST category/namespace delegation. The ticket was reopened for API-shape follow-ups, including namespace argument consistency and possible REST meta filtering.
tickets.documentation_rationale: The ticket was in the closed-query snapshot but is now reopened. Its final public API is not settled, so documentation classification and issue creation must wait until it closes again.

tickets.title: Abilities API: Expand `core/get-user-info` with profile fields and filtering
tickets.summary: Expands `core/get-user-info` to expose additional profile fields and introduces filtering of the returned user information.
tickets.documentation_rationale: Developer-facing expansion of a core ability and its extensibility belongs in a combined Abilities API Dev Note.

tickets.title: Abilities API: add `wp_ability_invoked` action for pipeline-entry observers
tickets.summary: Adds the `wp_ability_invoked` action at the ability invocation pipeline entry point so observers can monitor executions consistently.
tickets.documentation_rationale: A new public action needs hook parameters and timing documented, but it fits naturally in a combined Abilities API hooks Dev Note.

tickets.title: Docs: Standardize capitalization in @throws InvalidArgumentException annotations in WP_Ability_Category
tickets.summary: Adds a missing `@throws` annotation and normalizes capitalization in `WP_Ability_Category` PHPDoc.
tickets.documentation_rationale: Documentation-only cleanup with no runtime or public API change.

tickets.title: Abilities API: Duplicated $args variable in WP_Ability_Category::prepare_properties() PHPDoc
tickets.blocker: Trac ticket page could not be fetched during this run.

tickets.title: Abilities API: Unify schema conventions across core abilities
tickets.blocker: Trac returned HTTP 403 for the ticket page during this run.

tickets.title: WP_AI_Client_Ability_Function_Resolver::execute_abilities() answers non-ability function calls with a spurious error
tickets.blocker: Full ticket page could not be fetched during this run.

tickets.title: Abilities API: REST run requests pass untyped input to ability callbacks
